items.title: A* Pathfinding: The Math Behind Each Step
items.description: A slow walkthrough of A* pathfinding: each neighbor gets scored with f = g + h, then the lowest-cost frontier cell is expanded next.
items.notes: Matched to live upload titled A* Search Guide by duration and topic.

items.title: Breadth-First Search Explores the Grid
items.description: Breadth-first search expands outward layer by layer, guaranteeing the shortest path on an unweighted grid.

items.title: Pathfinding Cost Distribution Comparison
items.description: A* search, BFS, DFS, Dijkstra, and Greedy Best-First Search are tested across random grid worlds to compare their search costs.

items.title: Chess Bitboards: The 64-Bit Board Trick
items.description: A chess engine can store an entire board pattern inside one 64-bit integer, then generate pawn moves with fast bit operations.

items.title: How Stockfish Finds Rook Moves So Fast
items.description: Stockfish-style bitboards turn rook move generation into fast mask operations, table lookups, and fixed-shape CPU work instead of repeated board scanning.

items.title: Q-Learning: Before and After Training
items.description: A tabular reinforcement-learning agent starts with a weak policy, trains across episodes, and gradually learns better values for the grid.

items.title: Inside an LLM's Next-Token Choice
items.description: At each step, the model scores possible next tokens, turns those scores into probabilities, and samples one continuation.

items.description: Logit attribution highlights which previous tokens push the selected next token upward in the model's final score.

items.title: Sorting Cost Distribution Comparison
items.description: Sorting algorithms are tested on random lists so their best-case, worst-case, and ordinary shuffled-list costs can be compared on one graph.

items.description: A* search runs across random grid worlds, plotting how many cells it expands as grid size and wall layout vary.

items.title: Bubble Sort Cost Distribution on a Log Scale
items.description: Bubble sort samples are plotted on a logarithmic cost scale so the gap between linear and quadratic behavior is easier to see.

items.title: Bubble Sort Cost Distribution: N <= 100
items.description: Bubble sort is tested on shuffled lists up to 100 elements, showing the cloud of real operation counts between best and worst cases.

items.title: Bubble Sort Operation Cost Breakdown
items.description: Bubble sort runs with live meters for comparisons, reads, writes, swaps, and memory so the algorithm cost is visible as it sorts.

items.title: Cocktail Sort: Bidirectional Bubble Sort
items.description: Cocktail shaker sort moves in both directions through the list, bubbling small and large values toward their final positions.

items.title: Cocktail Sort Operation Cost Breakdown
items.description: Cocktail shaker sort is shown with live counters for comparisons, reads, writes, swaps, and memory usage.

items.title: Gnome Sort Operation Cost Breakdown
items.description: Gnome sort walks backward through local inversions, with operation meters showing the cost of each comparison and swap.

items.title: Insertion Sort Operation Cost Breakdown
items.description: Insertion sort builds a sorted prefix while live meters track comparisons, reads, writes, swaps, and memory use.

items.title: Merge Sort Operation Cost Breakdown
items.description: Merge sort recursively splits and merges while live meters show comparisons, reads, writes, swaps, and peak auxiliary memory.

items.title: Odd-Even Sort Operation Cost Breakdown
items.description: Odd-even sort alternates even and odd comparison passes, with live operation meters tracking the cost as the list sorts.

items.title: Selection Sort Operation Cost Breakdown
items.description: Selection sort repeatedly scans for the next minimum while live meters show comparisons, reads, writes, swaps, and memory use.

items.title: Two Sum: The Hash Map Trick
items.description: The Two Sum pattern is visualized from the naive pair scan to the optimized one-pass complement lookup using a hash map.

items.title: RL Learning Curves: Which Update Rule Learns Fastest?
items.description: A gridworld reinforcement-learning comparison: Q-learning, SARSA, Expected SARSA, and Monte Carlo control train on the same environment while their rolling episode returns reveal how quickly each method improves.

items.title: RL Feature Toggles: Which Training Trick Learns Fastest?
items.description: A reinforcement-learning gridworld test that starts with basic Q-learning, then toggles exploration decay, eligibility traces, and optimistic values while the greedy policy evaluation curve shows what each trick changes.

items.title: RL Feature Toggles in a Harder Gridworld
items.description: A larger reinforcement-learning gridworld test: basic Q-learning is compared against exploration decay, eligibility traces, and optimistic values while the learned greedy routes appear on the map.
